title: galium mollugo var. pallidulum
intro: Galium mollugo var. pallidulum, also known as pale bedstraw, is a variety of the Galium mollugo plant, a species of flowering plant in the bedstraw family. Historically, pale bedstraw has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to provide various health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties. Pale bedstraw can be prepared in various ways, including as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as diarrhea, dysentery, and menstrual cramps.
